observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the
Local Chief Executive (LCE)
agreed to require the (a) MT to (i) facilitate the prompt and
regular submission of the
CRAAF to the AT, and
completely signed RCDs to
the MAO; and (ii) notify the
MA of any identified errors
and adjustments made in the
RCD and cashbooks; (b) MA
to (i) thoroughly verify all
RCDs and supporting ORs
before recording them in the
books; (ii) implement a daily
recording system for
collections and deposits
instead of lumpsum entry at
month-end; (iii) prepare and
submit the BRS for bank
account under the GF, and
henceforth ensure that all
BRSs are submitted to the AT
in a timely manner; and (iv)
record all book reconciling
items to ensure an accurate
presentation of the CIB-LCCA
account balance.

Reiterated in Part II,
Observation No. 1

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the
LCE agreed to (a) create an
Inventory Committee to
handle the actual count of PPE
and submission of RPCPPE to
the AT; (b) direct the MA to (i)
draw the necessary adjusting
entries to reverse the
derecognition of fully
depreciated assets still in use
and prepare the IIRUP to
facilitate the disposal of
unserviceable PPE; and (ii)
record ongoing constructions
in CIP account and transfer
them to infrastructure PPE
account only upon updating of
completion; and (c) direct the
MT to ensure that all motor
vehicles owned by the
Municipality have updated
registrations with LTO and
insurance with GSIS.
observations.managementAction:
The reversion of the derecognition of fully depreciated assets was
completed.
(i) All ongoing construction projects are being recorded in the CIP for their transfer to infrastructure PPE account upon completion.
The MT has been directed to assign a point person for the LTO registration and GSIS insurance of all vehicles owned by the Municipality.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II,
Observation No. 2

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the
LCE agreed to (a) direct the
MA to (i) exert vigorous effort
to determine the details of long
outstanding balances in the
Due to NGAs account; (ii)
confirm the accuracy of the
balances of unliquidated fund
transfers by sending
confirmation letters,
coordinating with the source
agencies, and returning
unutilized balances, if
warranted; (iii) file a request
for authority to write off
dormant fund transfers to the
COA after the conditions and
requirements pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2016-005 have
been fully satisfied; and (iv)
ensure imposition of
liquidated damages on the
delayed project completion as
required by RA No. 9184; and
(b) direct the Municipal
Engineer (ME) to (i) submit
the approved contract time
extension; and (ii) closely
monitor the implementation
and completion of
infrastructure projects to
ensure adherence to programs
and timeline; and (c) continue
to satisfactorily implement the
PPAs funded by NGAs and
promptly return the unutilized
balances to their respective
sources.
observations.managementAction:
The LCE, MA, and other concerned personnel has exerted efforts but those involved are no longer in service.
The ME submitted the approved contract time extension and closely monitor the implementation and completion of infrastructure projects. The MA continue to satisfactorily implement the PPAs funded by NGAs and promptly return the unutilized balances to their respective sources.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the
LCE agreed to require the (a)
MT and MA to reconcile their
respective books to resolve the
noted discrepancy of
₱1,426,625.67 between the
recorded income from
business taxes vis-à-vis the
report submitted to the BLGF;
and (b) MT to (i) keep the
subsidiary ledgers of business
taxpayers updated to ensure
that all business
establishments settle their
business taxes yearly; (ii)
submit a report on delinquent
business taxpayers and
consider discussing with the
system developer any viable
way to include the said report
in the system; and (iii) enforce
available enforcement
measures to promote taxpayer
compliance with established
tax regulations.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation was not regularly completed due to lack of coordination and personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the
LCE agreed to (a) exhaust all
possible actions to enforce the
collection of real property tax
delinquencies, which may
include sending demand
letters, public awareness
campaigns, and streamlining
process, among others; and (b)
coordinate with the
Sangguniang Bayan (SB) and
the Local Finance Committee
(LFC) members to review
existing local policies and
regulations, identify areas for
improvement and implement
reforms to strengthen local tax
administration.

We recommended and the
LCE agreed to require the MA
to (a) review the amounts of
taxes withheld and (a) Not Implemented
remitted/posted to ensure
correctness of data and avoid
discrepancies; (b) immediately
remit the unremitted taxes (b) Implemented
withheld in the amount of
₱432,172.15 to the BIR; and
(c) practice intact remittance (c) Not Implemented
of taxes withheld to the BIR
within the prescribed period to
avoid the incurring surcharges
and penalties to be charged
against the erring employees.

We recommended and the
LCE agreed to require the MA
to (a) conduct a reconciliation
of records with the GSIS and
PHIC personnel regularly to
immediately address the
unremitted premiums of the
GSIS and PHIC members of
the Municipality; and (b) remit
within the prescribed period
the amount withheld to avoid
payment of penalty that may
be imposed by the same
agencies.

1.20 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to require the
(a) MA to: (i) recognize in the books the reconciling items affecting the CLT and
CIB-LCCA accounts; (ii) prepare BRSs for the three bank account with unreconciled
balances, which totaled ₱8,236,539.98; (iii) revert the amount of unreleased and stale
checks totaling ₱2,193,139.67 to the CIB-LCCA account; and (iv) reconcile the CIB-
LCCA account balance with its SLs; (b) MT to: (i) prepare the RUC and SSC at year-
end and furnish the same to the MA for appropriate adjustments in the books to
reflect the accurate CIB-LCCA account balance; and (ii) supervise and oversee the
preparation of RCDs, CRAAF and RAAF to address the noted deficiencies; and (c)
MA and MT to: (i) reconcile their respective cash records to resolve the differences
noted; and (ii) henceforth, ensure the accurate and reliable presentation of cash
account balances.

2.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to: (a) direct the Inventory Committee
to perform a complete physical count of all properties owned and controlled by the
Municipality and reconcile the results with the accounting records; (b) require the
MT, as the Property Custodian, to maintain PCs and LRNPC; (c) instruct the
Chairman of the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the Property Unit and
Municipal Engineering Office to prepare and submit the a complete inventory of all
Local Roads, the corresponding Road Map and the RPCLRN; and (d) direct the MA
to: (i) maintain complete PPELCs, including LRNLC; and (ii) provide the required
disclosure in the NFS for the Road Network account.

3.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MA to recognize the
accrued liability from earned leave benefits at yearend; and (b) HRMO Officer to
regularly furnish the MAO a summary or report on earned leave credits.

6.9 We recommended and the LCE, as the head of the Municipal Development
Council, agreed to direct its members to: (a) continue adhering to the proper
allocation and utilization of the 20% DF; and (b) ensure implementation and
completion of all programmed PPAs within set timeline to ensure the prompt
realization of benefits for the intended beneficiaries.

7.19 We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MBO Officer and MA to: (i)
reconcile the discrepancies noted in the audit related to the utilization of the DRRMF;
and (ii) henceforth, ensure the correctness of the monthly Report on Sources and
Utilization of the DRRMF; (b) BAC Secretariat to: (i) post in the PhilGEPS the RFQ
and Award for the procurements covered by the alternative mode of procurement –
Shopping; (c) MA, BAC Chairperson, and the MDRRM Officer to ensure that all
documents supporting payment claims are valid and complete before payment is made to
suppliers; and (d) MA to: (i) submit on a timely manner the Report on Sources and
Utilization of the DRRMF; and (ii) make the necessary adjustments to ensure the
accurate presentation of the TL-DRRMF account balance.

8.17. We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the HRMO to: (a) ensure
continued compliance with applicable law, rules, and regulations on personnel
management, as well as the submission of the minimum documentary requirements
prescribed under Section 1.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012;
(b) strengthen internal controls over payroll funds for Casual, JO, and COS
employees by implementing an ATM-based payroll system; and (c) consider the
adoption of an automated system for monitoring and recording attendance to
promote punctuality and support more efficient payroll processing.

9.13. We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE (a) exhaust all
possible means to enforce the collection of real property tax delinquencies, including
public awareness campaigns, and streamlining process, among others; and (b)
coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) and the Local Finance Committee
(LFC) members to review existing local policies and regulations, identify areas for
improvement. and implement reforms to strengthen local tax administration.

9.14. We also recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the: (a) MTO and MAO
to reconcile their records in reporting the Income and Expenses of the Municipality;
(b) MTO to provide the MAO a copy of Certified List of All Property Tax
Delinquencies as of year-end; and (c) MAO to make the necessary adjustment on the
RPT Receivable and SET Receivable Account.

10.9. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the GAD Focal Point Person
to continue to: (a) comply with the budgetary requirement of at least 5% of the
Municipality’s total budget appropriation for GAD PPAs; (b) prioritize the
implementation of the identified PPAs as per GPB to achieve and promote desirable
gender responsive society; (c) utilize fully the GAD budget in order to attain the
objective for which the funds were appropriated; and (d) coordinate with the MBO
for the assignment/creation of a separate RC.

11.7. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to: (a) review the
amounts of taxes withheld and remitted/posted to ensure correctness of data and
avoid discrepancies; (b) immediately remit the unremitted taxes withheld in the
amount of ₱32,710.13 to the BIR; and (c) practice intact remittance of taxes withheld
to the BIR within the prescribed period to avoid the incurring surcharges and
penalties to be charged against the erring employees.

12.13. We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation, and the LCE agreed to
require the MA to: (a) conduct regular reconciliation of records with the GSIS,
HDMF, and PHIC personnel to promptly address the unremitted premiums of the
Municipality’s GSIS, HDMF, and PHIC members; and (b) remit the withheld
amounts within the prescribed period to avoid penalties that may be imposed by these
agencies.

13.5. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to exhaust all efforts
to settle expeditiously the outstanding suspensions and be more prudent in the
exercise of their functions to minimize, if not eliminate, audit suspensions,
disallowances and charges.

We recommended and the
LCE agreed to direct the MA
to (a) strictly comply with the
requirements and guidelines
provided in COA-DBM-

advance voucher with

adequate planning and close

the fund to attain substantial

We recommended and the

station, and fully operate the

We recommended and the

We reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant to (a)  review and analyze the dormant accounts,  and to locate the documents and the debtors of these dormant and long-outstanding accounts; (b) send collection letters to the debtors; (c) if efforts fail and the settlement of these accounts would  be nil, request authority to write-off the dormant accounts from the Commission on Audit; and (d) reclassify the dormant and long-outstanding balances as non-current assets  in order to present the reliable balances of the Receivable  accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) Municipal Accountant  to (i) recognize the receipt of procured inventories by  using the asset account; and (ii) maintain a subsidiary ledger  for each type of supplies, crops and other inventory items, to record acquisition, issuance and disposal; (b) create a Committee on Inventory who shall be tasked to conduct a periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials at least once a year and to be reported in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to be submitted to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year; and (c) direct  the Municipal Treasurer/Supply and Property Officer to (i) maintain stock cards  to record all receipts and issuances of supplies; and (ii)  submit to the Municipal Accountant the  Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued supported with the requisition and issue slips.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to (i)  to reconcile and retrieve the supporting documents evidencing the ownership of the recorded forwarded balances of the Land Account totaling to ₱40,724,413.66; (ii) to maintain PPELC and PC for each category of PPE;  and (iii)create a Committee on Inventory   to conduct the annual physical inventory of Property, Plant and Equipment; and   (b) Municipal  Accountant to  coordinate with  the Information Technology (IT) provider of  the Electronic National Government Accounting System (E-NGAS) on how to generate the depreciation of Infrastructure Assets and reflect adjustments in the  carrying value of the said PPE account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  require the  (a)  Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management ( MDRRM) Officer to take charge of the  implementation of the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) under LDRRMF; (b)  Municipal Accountant and MDRRM Officer  to submit within  the prescribed period the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization to the Audit Team for a timely review of the projects and activities; and (c) Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to fill up  the vacant positions that will assist the MDRRMO.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MENRO,  Municipal Administrator, Municipal Engineer and  Municipal Accountant to (a) (i) require the employees in charge of operations in the transfer station to maintain a logbook or basic recordkeeping of the dump trucks of the contractor’s ingress and egress; and (ii) attach the trip tickets of the contractor and truck scale weight report issued by the landfill to the detailed Statement of Account; and (b) submit the deficient documents to prevent the suspension of the above transactions.

and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the complete technical specifications of

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to (a) submit detailed/specific PPAs and secure approval from the Sangguniang Bayan for  the reprogramming of programs, projects, and activities of appropriated and disbursed contracts entered into in connection with LDRRMF totaling ₱19,807,897.68.00, which were realigned and spent for COVID 19 programs, projects and activities pursuant to Section 305g of R.A. No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 2012-002; (b) refrain from incurring expenditures for  programs, projects and activities beyond their appropriations; and (c) seek authority and approval from the Sangguniang Bayan before the implementation of programs, projects and activities (PPAs)  in cases when  PPAs are presented  in the Budget and Annual Investment Plan /Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Investment Plan (LDDRMFIP) in lump-sum appropriations and in generic terms pursuant to  the local government’s principle of check and balance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Municipal Accountant to (i) submit the documentary requirements to support the emergency procurement made from various suppliers; (b) the concerned officials to comply with the posting requirement pursuant to GPPB Resolutions No. 18-2020 and to adopt  competitive public bidding in the procurement of goods and services; and (c) submit the report on the distribution list of commodities during the pandemic.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, together with the authorized LGU representative, the MSWD Officer and other concerned officials  to (a) impose stricter measures in the profiling and monitoring of payouts to the beneficiaries specifically in times of calamities such as conduct of strict house to house screening and taking photos of the beneficiary during payouts in accordance with the requirements of DSWD MC No. 9 series of 2020; and (b) submit complete  and valid  documentary requirements to support all  fund disbursements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  require  all concerned departments/offices to (a) submit the deficient documents to validate the propriety and validity of the payments made for Hazard Pay; and (b) desist from the payment of hazard pay and other allowances relative to COVID 19 outbreak which runs counter to the provisions of  applicable laws, rules, and regulations and not  supported with proper and adequate documentation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the concerned implementing officials to optimally utilize the 20% DF in accordance with the development plan of the Municipality; (b) require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documentary requirements to support the payment on the transactions made under 20%; and (c) the Municipal Engineer to require contractors to comply with the required specifications on signboards/billboards to be posted at all venues where the Municipality's PPAs will be implemented in accordance with the government's full disclosure policy.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to (a) require payees to submit complete supporting documents in payment of disbursements; and (b) submit the Report of SEF Utilization every quarter within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive increase the fidelity bond of the Municipal Treasurer commensurate to her fund accountabilities in accordance with Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 in order to provide adequate protection on the Municipality’s resources.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to (a) ensure that the amount of remittance to concerned regulatory agencies is equal to the amounts withheld; and (b) remit any verified underpayment to the appropriate agency keeping in mind that any penalty and other charges imposed by other agencies becomes a personal liability of the negligent employee or official concerned.

observations.managementAction:
The Treasury department already maintained index card/ledgers for each taxpayer and updated it on a regular basis.

The Treasury Dept.  already established the accurate amount of basic RPT and SET receivable from each tax payer as of end of CY 2018 and already furnished the Accounting office a duly certified list of taxpayers with actual due and collectible for the year.

The management is planning to utilize the existing program/system being used by the treasury dept. to streamline the establishment of accurate amount of collectible from each taxpayer. observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that Management (a) form an inventory committee that will conduct an annual count of all PPE items including the preparation and submission of the inventory report/RPCPPE; and (b) perform constant follow up with the offices concerned to facilitate the immediate registration of several vehicles and titling of the several lots in the name of the Municipality. We further recommended that Management provide adequate appropriation and budget for the required costs/fees for the registration and titling of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality.

We  reiterated our previous years’ recommendation  that  Management (a) direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor and Treasurer in establishing the accurate amount of basic RPT and SET receivable from each taxpayer as of  the end of 2017; (b) require the Accounting Office to set up and maintain updated index card to facilitate the establishment of the accurate amount of collectible from each taxpayer; (c) recognize the journal entry voucher to adjust the amount of recorded receivables to reconcile the general ledger balance with the amount actually collectible from each taxpayer based on the certified list of collectibles and reflected in the taxpayer’s ledger; and (d) direct the  Municipal  Treasurer  to invariably furnish  the  Municipal  Accountant  a  duly  certified  list showing  the  names  of  the  taxpayers  and  the  actual  due  and  collectible  for  the  year  as basis in  setting  up  at the beginning of the year, the  Real Property Tax  and  Special Education Tax  receivables due for the year.

We recommended that Management instruct the Local Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to record the correcting entries for the transfer of the unexpended balance to the Special Trust Fund, disclose the details of the unexpended balance of the DRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements to provide adequate information to stakeholders about the Fund and maintain a subsidiary record of the Trust Liability-DRRMF account by source and by year.

We recommended that Management (a) comply with the provisions set forth by COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the preparation and submission of approved estimates of petty expenses for one month as additional documentary requirement which should be the basis for initial cash advances/set-up of petty cash fund and to ensure that the amount of the PCF is reasonable to meet the petty expenses intended for the fund; and (b) assess the established amount required for PCF and monitor its utilization in order to regulate the amount necessary for the agency’s petty expenditures.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that Management (a) exert efforts to  provide a central storage room for its supply requirements for stocking purposes; (b) strictly observe the prescribed accounting guidelines and control procedures on the receipt, issuance, and storage/custodianship of supplies and materials; and (c) pending the availability of a central storage area for  inventories, install adequate controls by designating a “supplies custodian” and maintaining a record of receipt and issuances to pave the way for establishing the cost of ending inventory at the end of the accounting period.

We recommended that Management (a) prepare the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) annually to present the specific projects and activities to be funded out of the 70% allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness, the lump-sum of 30% for Quick Response Fund (QRF) and the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of the previous years; and (b) require the LDRRMO, through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC), to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to be certified correct by the Local Accountant and submitted to the COA Auditor of the LGU on or before the 15th day after the end of each month for the timely review of the projects and activities.

We recommended that Management require the Solid Waste Management Board to devote time and attention in the preparation of the SWMP in order to integrate the latest solid waste management plans and strategies of the Municipality and its barangays to ensure its sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international developments in the field of solid waste management.

We recommended that the Local Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with other offices involved to (a) work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies; (b) make payments of supplies and materials directly to supplier and limit payments through reimbursement; and (c) ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the disbursement vouchers before their processing and payment.

We recommended Management to stop the practice of putting billboards/signboards bearing the names and images of the local officials on PPAs and comply with the aforementioned regulation to disclose all transactions involving PPAs for transparency and accountability.

We recommended that Management stop the further payments of allowances to national government officials until the Sangguniang Bayan pass a resolution authorizing the grant of allowances and specifying the rates of additional allowances to these officials in accordance with the inherent conditions under Section 447(a) (1) (xi) of Republic Act No. 7160 and Local Budget Circular No. 53 dated September 1, 1993.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) direct all offices concerned and the BAC Secretariat to prepare a complete Annual Procurement Plan containing all the requirements of the Municipality, procurement schedule and mode of procurement; and (b) direct the BAC to (i) procure its common-use supplies requirements through the Procurement Service of the DBM; (ii) subject all procurements to public bidding unless the conditions are met for the use of shopping and small value procurement as alternative mode of procurement; and (iii) to enhance the transparency of the procurement process, ensure the widest dissemination of procurement-related notices and strictly adhere to the advertising and posting requirements of the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184.
We recommended that Management make payments directly to supplier and limit making payments through reimbursement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the: a) BAC Secretariat to submit all advance copies of perfected contracts, purchase orders and pre-repair evaluation report, and their supporting documents to the Audit Team within the prescribed period; and b) the Officer responsible for accepting deliveries to notify COA of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries for ocular verification of the existence and condition of the supplies, materials and equipment pursuant to the above stated provisions of COA Circulars No. 2009-001, 2009-002 and 95-006.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer: (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to cause delay in the verification of accounts and conduct of post-audit activities; and (b) observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team. Otherwise, cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission.

We recommended that Management require the: (a) Municipal Planning Development Coordinator, Municipal Budget Officer and other key officials involved in the preparation of the budget, to  prepare a reliable AIP to serve its purpose as a planning and budgetary control tool; and (b) Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Engineer and other implementing offices strictly utilize the 20% Development Fund to only eligible capital expenditures and authorized PPAs that have undergone priority ranking procedures in terms of urgency and impact.

We recommended  that the Local Chief Executive (a) through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes; (b) implement the plan on the establishment of a functional Municipal MRF; (c) address the issue on the controlled and open dumpsites and  implement  their closure or eventual phase out in accordance with the guidelines set by the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000; and  (d)  fully implement the No Littering Ordinance by assigning an officer/office  who will monitor the implementation of the ordinance and setting up  monitoring mechanisms  such as installation of CCTV in public places.

We recommended that the Municipality (a) develop marketing strategies to encourage vendors, investors and the constituents to patronize the market and slaughterhouse in order to increase revenues that could be generated therefrom; and (b) reassign market personnel to other departments/service units wherein their services could be more useful and necessary to minimize the expenses on personal services charged to the market and slaughterhouse operations.

We recommended that Management (a) be more prudent in the exercise of their fiscal functions in order to minimize, if not eliminate, audit suspensions, disallowances and charges in the future; and (b) cause the refund of disallowed payments immediately in order to avoid filing of appropriate charges against the persons liable.
We recommended and Management agreed to comply strictly with the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding of Final VAT and EWT from payments to utility companies.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the Municipal Accountant: (a) exert extra effort to immediately trace back the source of discrepancies noted under the Due to Other Agencies accounts by obtaining Statement of account of each employee from these agencies to determine the underpayment of premiums and loans and compare them with payrolls and schedule of remittances and effect the necessary adjustments; and (b) cause the remittance of any verified underpayment to the appropriate agency keeping in mind that any penalty and other charges imposed by other agencies becomes a personal liability of the negligent employee or official concerned.

We recommended that Management exert more effort to comply with the GAD mechanisms and processes in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 or Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women.
